summary: Tight first set, strong Terrapin Station, solid mid‑tempo flow
blurb: The show delivers a high‑energy first set with standout Jack Straw, Althea and Peggy‑O, while the second set shines on Bertha > Cumberland Blues, Looks Like Rain and an inspired Terrapin Station. Brent adds brass‑like textures in the second set and the rhythm section stays hot throughout.
review: September 20, 1988 at Madison Square Garden finds the Dead firing on all cylinders. The opening set kicks off with a crisp Jack Straw that showcases Jerry’s tight solo work, followed by a delightfully phrased Althea and a soaring Peg­gy‑O that lets Brent Mydland sparkle on the master‑piece tease. The second half builds momentum through a rare Bertha > Cumberland Blues segue, a soulful Looks Like Rain, and a Terrapin Station that’s bolstered by brass/woodwind‑like keyboard textures. While Louie Louie and the closing Truckin’/Morning Dew stretch the set’s energy to its peak, a few of the later numbers (Louie Louie, GDTRFB) lose a bit of focus for some listeners. Overall, the performance is solid, with Phil’s bass sitting front‑and‑center and the dual drummers keeping a steady, sweaty groove.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five recordings label the show as positive, praising standout songs (Jack Straw, Terrapin Station, Bertha>Cumberland Blues) and solid band playing; one mixed review calls it "well played if unremarkable" but offers no criticism of performance. Consistent high praise across multiple sources justifies a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard capture with clear, well‑balanced mix; highlights include Peggy‑O, Looks Like Rain and Terrapin Station while maintaining full show integrity.

key_highlights: Terrapin Station enriched by Brent’s brass/woodwind texture
Bertha > Cumberland Blues segue that keeps the momentum rolling
High‑octane Louie Louie into a climactic Truckin'/Morning Dew sequence

band_performance.Jerry: Delightful vocal phrasing on Althea and tight, clean solos on Jack Straw and Bertha; overall solid guitar work throughout the night.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is really good and prominently heard, anchoring the band’s hot, sweaty groove for the entire set.
band_performance.Brent: Adds brass‑like textures during the second‑set Terrapin Station, shines on the Peggy‑O/Masterpiece tease, and generally holds his own with shifting‑gear keyboard work.
band_performance.Billy: Drums stay solid with no major complaints; rhythm section maintains a steady drive despite one minor post‑drum comment.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
